channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Protesters storm and destroy a family home of Bangladesh's ousted Prime Minister Hasina
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Thousands of protesters in Bangladesh took out their anger at exiled former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina on Wednesday by destroying a family home that came to symbolize the country's independence — and now, they say, the authoritarianism they believe she led.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The sea was once a blessing for the Pakistani city of Gwadar. But it's become a curse
item.description: GWADAR, Pakistan (AP) — There was a time when few people in the coastal Pakistani city of Gwadar understood what climate change was. After a decade of extreme weather, many more do. Rain battered Gwadar for almost 30 consecutive hours last February.
item.dc:creator: RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico deploys the first of 10,000 National Guard troops to US border after Trump's tariff threat
item.description: A line of Mexican National Guard and Army trucks are rumbling along the border separating Ciudad Juárez and El Paso, Texas, among the first of 10,000 troops Mexico has sent to its northern frontier following tariff threats by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Megan Janetsky, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukraine wants partner countries to join in postwar development worth billions
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine wants to collaborate with partner countries on postwar projects worth billions of dollars not just in mining rare earth elements, but also in energy and construction sectors to help rebuild the country, Ukrainian Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Argentina's president bans gender-affirming care for people under age 18
item.description: BUENOS AIRES (AP) — Argentina’s president has signed a decree banning gender-affirming care for people under age 18, a presidential spokesperson said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump’s demand that US aid workers return home sparks outrage in Washington and anxiety overseas
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Frustration boiled over Wednesday among supporters of the United States' lead aid agency at a Washington rally, and anxious aid workers abroad scrambled to pack up households after the Trump administration abruptly pulled almost all agency staffers off the job and out of the field.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, MATTHEW LEE and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Guatemala to accept migrants from other countries deported by US
item.description: Guatemalan President Bernardo Arévalo said Wednesday that his country has agreed to accept migrants from other countries after they are deported from the United States. At that point, under the “safe third country” agreement announced on Wednesday, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: White House: Trump hasn't committed to sending US military to Gaza
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt on Wednesday insisted President Trump has not committed to sending the U.S. military into Gaza after he appeared to float such a possibility during a joint press conference with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu the day before.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: From fighting disease to protecting the Amazon rainforest, USAID has big impact across the globe
item.description: The Trump administration's decision to close the U.S. Agency for International Development has drawn widespread criticism from congressional Democrats and raised questions about the influence billionaire ally Elon Musk wields over the federal government.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Earthquakes are rattling Greece's volcanic island of Santorini: What we know
item.description: Although Greece is no stranger to earthquakes, seismic activity of such frequency and intensity without having been preceded by a major quake is unusual, seismologists say.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A special tribunal to prosecute Russian leaders over Ukraine wins backing from European institutions
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — A project to establish a court to prosecute the Russian leaders who orchestrated the invasion of Ukraine took a step forward Wednesday, with an announcement from a group of international organizations, including the European Union and the Council of Europe, working together with Ukra
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL and RAF CASERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's Gaza plan has stunned the region. Here's a look at the serious obstacles it faces
item.description: President Donald Trump's stunning proposal to forcibly transfer hundreds of thousands of Palestinians out of the Gaza Strip and develop it as a tourist destination faces major obstacles.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Witnesses describe chaos and bloodshed in Sweden’s worst mass shooting
item.description: They scrambled for cover as quickly as they could as shots rang out, sheltering behind and under whatever they could find to escape the gunman and the gore. One survivor with children feared she might never see them again.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump invites Indian PM Modi to White House
item.description: President Trump has invited Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi to the White House next week for a working visit, a White House official confirmed to The Hill. Modi will travel to Washington, D.C., for a two-day visit. The invite comes just days after a recent phone call between the two leaders.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, The Hill

item.title: U.S. allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: Both U.S. allies and adversaries alike rejected President Donald Trump’s suggestion that the U.S. “take over” the war-torn Gaza Strip and turn it into the “Riviera of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: USAID is going away. Here's what it's been doing in South America
item.description: The dismantling of the U.S. Agency for International Development will deliver a major blow to efforts including humanitarian assistance in Colombia, conservation efforts in Brazil and coca eradication in Peru — South American countries that have been a priority for the support.
item.dc:creator: Fabiano Maisonnave, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels have seized another town in eastern Congo
item.description: Officials and residents say Rwanda-backed rebels have gained ground in eastern Congo despite the unilateral ceasefire they declared earlier this week, taking control of a town 100 kilometers (60 miles) from the provincial capital of Bukavu.
item.dc:creator: Justin Kabumba, Jean-Yves Kamale And Monika Pronczuk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UN chief sounds the alarm amid fears over possible DOGE-inspired cuts after Trump's order
item.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres sends a letter to U.N. staff as President Donald Trump makes sweeping changes to U.S. foreign aid and funding.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: USPS lifts suspension of Hong Kong and China parcels
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service reversed course Wednesday, saying it would continue to accept all inbound mail and packages from China and Hong Kong.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico says it will not allow US to send Mexican migrants to Guantanamo Bay
item.description: Mexico told the the U.S. government it will not allow Mexican migrants to be sent to the Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, detention camp, the country's foreign minister said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thailand cuts power supplies to Myanmar border towns in effort to curb scam rings
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thailand on Wednesday cut off power supplies to areas in Myanmar where scam networks operate, following a public outcry demanding government action to tackle the problem.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Saudi Arabia contradicts Trump, vows no ties with Israel without creation of Palestinian state
item.description: Saudi Arabia said it would not establish ties with Israel unless a Palestinian state is created, contradicting a suggestion from U.S. President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Philippine Vice President Duterte impeached over Marcos assassination remark and other allegations
item.description: Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte has been impeached on a range of accusations that include plotting to assassinate the president and engaging in large-scale corruption.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Greek island Santorini evacuated after earthquakes shake tourist destination
item.description: Hundreds of earthquakes that have rattled the Greek islands since last week have prompted the evacuation of thousands from the tourist hotspot of Santorini.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PM declares 'worst mass shooting in Swedish history' after gunman kills 10 on school campus
item.description: Swedish police are responding to a school shooting in Orebro, Sweden, on Tuesday after reports said at least 10 people were killed
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexican cartels targeting Border Patrol agents with kamikaze drones, explosives amid Trump crackdown: report
item.description: Mexican drug cartels have planned attacks on U.S. Border Patrol agents with kamikaze drones and other explosives to combat a crackdown by the Trump administration at the southern border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio visiting Central America with the Panama Canal and immigration top of mind
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio is on his first foreign trip in office, arriving in Central America on Saturday to press President Donald Trump's top priority — curbing illegal immigration — and to bring the message that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Gaza ceasefire sees its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — The ceasefire in Gaza saw its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners on Saturday, and the crucial Rafah border crossing reopened two days before discussions on the truce’s far more difficult second phase begin.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and IMAD ISSEID, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Hostage releases begin in Gaza Strip
item.description: Hamas handed captive Israeli soldier Agam Berger over to the Red Cross, the first of eight hostages set to be released today.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation
